questions.question: What is "alignment debt" in post-hoc VLM training?
options: Unpaid compute bills from alignment research
A text-pretrained LLM never natively learned visual tokens — bolting on vision afterward forces it to relate modalities without forgetting text, leaving measurable damage
The cost of RLHF licensing
Misaligned tensor shapes between towers
questions.explanation: LLaVA, BLIP-2, Qwen-VL, and Idefics all follow the post-hoc recipe. The debt shows in three documented symptoms — quantified in MM1.5 and hinted at in OneVision's ablations.

questions.question: What are the three measurable symptoms of alignment debt?
options: Slow inference, large checkpoints, high VRAM

Catastrophic forgetting (GSM8K drops 5-10 points), answer drift (rephrased visual questions get different answers), and visual-text inconsistency (describing an image correctly then contradicting the description)
Overfitting, underfitting, and dead neurons
Token leaks, NaN losses, and OOM errors
questions.explanation: The inconsistency symptom is the most telling: visual tokens don't participate in the LLM's internal consistency checks the way its own text tokens do — weaker bindings from post-hoc attachment.

questions.question: How does InternVL3's native pretraining differ, and what corpus mix does it use?
options: It trains vision first, then text
One pretraining run with text and multimodal interleaved from step one: ~40% text-only, 35% interleaved image-text, 20% caption pairs, 5% video — no alignment stage, no forgetting to recover from
It uses 100% synthetic images
It skips pretraining entirely
questions.explanation: Visual tokens are first-class citizens from the first gradient step. The result: matching Gemini 2.5 Pro on MMMU-Pro at 78B params, open — the strongest argument yet against post-hoc bolting.

questions.question: How does V2PE differ from Qwen2-VL's M-RoPE?
options: It abandons rotary embeddings

Position encoding varies per modality (1D text, 2D image, 3D video) with LEARNABLE hidden-dim allocation per frequency band, instead of M-RoPE's fixed axis split — worth 1-2 video-benchmark points at equal compute
It only supports square images
It is M-RoPE renamed
questions.explanation: Same RoPE frequency base, but the trade-off between temporal and spatial frequency resolution becomes a learned parameter. Not a revolution — "cleaner," as the lesson puts it.

questions.question: Why does the text-only share (~40%) of the native corpus matter so much?
options: Text data is cheapest to store
The model must remain a frontier text reasoner — native pretraining only beats post-hoc if text skills never degrade, so pure text keeps majority-scale presence in the same loss
Regulators require text quotas
Text tokens train faster than image tokens
questions.explanation: The whole point is avoiding the post-hoc forgetting tax. Interleaved and caption data teach cross-modal binding, but the text foundation that sets the reasoning ceiling (per the recipes lesson) must be protected inside the single run.
